name: Aluminum
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; covers the 7 June cutoff -> early 10 June; 8/9 June runs empty). **THE SIGNAL OF THE DAY: ALUMINUM (LME) $3,517.50, -9.4% 1D, -2.1% 1M, +5.9% 3M — A RISK-PREMIUM UNWIND, NOT A DEMAND COLLAPSE; THE MOST REVERSIBLE MOVE ON THE BOARD BECAUSE THE UNDERLYING PREMIUM IS REAL.** Aluminum was the ONE commodity in the complex carrying a LEGITIMATE PHYSICAL war premium — the Iranian strikes on EGA Al-Taweelah (Abu Dhabi) power infrastructure + ALBA Bahrain Lines 1-3 offline since mid-March genuinely removed Gulf smelter supply, lifting it to a 4-year high ~$3,790/t on 2 June. The June 8 Israel-Iran mutual stand-down removed the acute fear of a FURTHER supply hit -> a crowded 4-yr-high long met that relief + dollar strength (DXY 99.9) -> a sharp -9.4% liquidation. CRITICAL: this is a RISK-PREMIUM UNWIND, NOT demand destruction (3M still +5.9%; copper resilient at $6.27/-2.2% 1M) — and it was as much a DOLLAR event (DXY 99.9, the metals-killer) as a war event. Because the underlying premium is REAL (genuine removed Gulf smelter capacity, not sentiment), this is the MOST REVERSIBLE move in the graph: a re-hit smelter / confirmed Hormuz mining / an ALBA-EGA non-restart would re-price aluminum violently upward. DATA CONFIDENCE: the -9.4% rests on thinly-printed yfinance ALI=F — treat the precise figure as +/-2pts; direction and driver are corroborated. Aluminum is the cleanest PHYSICAL-supply proxy in the graph. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: the existing '>25% Gulf production drop (ALBA+EGA)' trigger STAYS watching. NEW WATCHING trigger added: 'ALBA/EGA confirmed non-restart OR a Gulf smelter re-hit -> aluminum re-prices violently upward' — the cleanest physical-supply proxy in the graph. PRIOR — Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session -> 5-6 June). **ALUMINUM $3,950.25/mt (-1.2% 1D, +12.9% 1M, +18.7% 3M) — THE EGA/HORMUZ WAR PREMIUM PERSISTS ON THE MONTH, WHILE THE -1.2% ON THE DAY WAS PART OF THE 5-JUNE MACRO METALS LIQUIDATION (RATES/RISK-OFF), NOT A WAR MOVE.** Dual read: (1) STRUCTURAL — the EGA/Hormuz war premium is intact and durable on the 1M (+12.9%) and 3M (+18.7%) legs (EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart + the Guinea bauxite-export-control overlay remain the floor). (2) MACRO — the -1.2% on the day was part of the broad metals liquidation in the 5-June RATES/AI risk-off (Broadcom miss + hot May jobs -> real-yield + dollar bid -> gold -3.1%, oil -2%, VIX +39.7%), NOT a war risk-off; the war eased on the margin this window (oil down on diplomacy; the 5-6 June Gulf exchange was zero-casualty/calibrated). So today's print is macro-driven liquidation layered on a still-elevated structural Hormuz premium — re-escalation (a non-calibrated Iranian act, a Beirut strike) re-bids the premium fast; aluminum remains a clean Hormuz/Gulf war-premium barometer (markets E4). PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE WAR-PREMIUM BLEW OUT — ALUMINUM -7.4% 1D AS THE 4-YEAR-HIGH GULF/HORMUZ PREMIUM DEFLATED ON DE-ESCALATION. A REAL MOVE, NOT A YFINANCE STALE-COMPARATOR ARTIFACT.** LME aluminum -7.4% to ~$3,700/mt in a single session. The chain (~75% crisis): futures hit a 4-year high $3,680/mt on 1 June on 'prolonged Middle East supply disruption' (Gulf smelter / EGA Hormuz-export premium + power-cost fears) -> the 4 June Gulf DE-ESCALATION (no new Iran barrage, oil -1%, VIX to 15) deflated the war/Hormuz premium that drove the spike -> a crowded 4-year-high long unwound violently. Secondary (~25%): classic blow-off-top profit-taking; LME inventories actually TIGHTENING (-0.58%), so this is PREMIUM UNWIND, not demand destruction. **IMPORTANT for the Graph Engineer (markets C2): unlike several prior sessions this -7.4% is a REAL move, NOT a yfinance stale-comparator artifact** — the 1-Jun 4-yr-high ($3,680) and the de-escalation catalyst both corroborate it; do NOT flag as artifact. This is the SAME signature logged on 15 May (-8.9%) and mid-May — aluminum is now behaving as a clean Hormuz/Gulf war-premium BAROMETER in the metals complex (markets E4): re-escalation (a non-calibrated Iranian act, a Beirut strike) re-bids the premium fast. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline (~12-month restart) + the Guinea bauxite-export-control overlay remain the structural floor under any premium unwind. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **ALUMINUM +13% MoM / +26% 3M — A DUAL AI/GRID-DEMAND + RESIDUAL GULF-PREMIUM BID, MOSTLY NON-CRISIS. LME aluminum $3,917.75/mt (+0.2% 1D, +13.0% MoM, +26.0% 3M). The move is ~75% structural (AI/grid power demand, the same capex story lifting copper +8.2% MoM) and ~25% a residual EGA/Gulf supply premium that has NOT fully unwound even as oil de-prices. Unlike the fertilizer/LNG fast legs, aluminum's premium is demand-led, so it is not unwinding on deal-anticipation.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **STALENESS CORRECTION: the prior (May 19) summary read aluminum as soft/falling toward $3,400 on China-demand skepticism — that is OUT OF DATE. ALUMINUM IS NOW AT A 4-YEAR HIGH ~$3,640-3,680 (+0.4% 1D, +2.6% MoM, +17.4% 3M). The yfinance -13% 1D print is the recurring LME-vintage / stale-comparator ARTIFACT (LME closed/continuous-contract roll) — DISCARD it, exactly as on May 19-20.** DUAL DRIVER (roughly 50/50): (a) CRISIS — the Mideast naval blockade halts Gulf exports = ~9% of global aluminum supply offline (EGA Al Taweelah, ALBA, Borouge); (b) NON-CRISIS SUPPLY SHOCK — Guinea announced bauxite export controls starting June (bauxite is the upstream feedstock for alumina→aluminum). The Guinea overlay is the key intelligence: part of the strength is now structural and PERSISTS even on a Gulf/Hormuz deal — a deal removes the ~50% crisis premium but NOT the Guinea bauxite-control leg. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline (~12-month restart) remains the structural floor. PRIOR (May 19): Day 80 (Monday). **C8 ALUMINUM ~FLAT ACTUAL MONDAY ($3,503.50 vs Friday $3,511 = ~-0.2%); yfinance -7.6% IS STALE-COMPARATOR ARTIFACT (same artifact flagged for WTI).** Friday May 15 move was -9.8% premium unwind on Trump-Xi summit Hormuz-reopen optics + 4-year-high long-positioning blow-out + EGA Hormuz-premium unwind = the cycle-level event; Monday is consolidation. Underlying drivers per LME / Discovery Alert / westmetall: weak early-session spot purchasing sentiment + profit-taking after Q1 rally; softening industrial output indicators in major consuming regions Q2 2026; China social inventory at 5-year period high (inventory buildup cycle not ended); LME inventories fell another 0.72% but Chinese hubs (Wuxi) active destocking cushioned downside. **Assessment**: Friday's -9.8% was position-unwind on Trump-Xi optics; Monday's consolidation says market is NOT extrapolating Hormuz reopening despite Trump rhetoric. **If Tuesday produces strike order, aluminum snaps back +3-5% to ~$3,680; if Tuesday produces back-off, aluminum continues weak toward $3,400.** The asymmetry is real — physical Hormuz remains constrained even with Trump-Xi joint-statement language. **EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor.** **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICE IS FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSE $3,511/mt per markets dossier Section A** (-9.8% 1D, -3.7% 1M, +18.2% 3M vs pre-war ~$2,650). Per markets dossier C6: **EGA Hormuz-premium 4-year-high long blown out** + Trump-Xi joint statement Hormuz language + UAE-side rerouting (Modi-MBZ MoUs include ADNOC SPR + Indian crude at Fujairah outside Hormuz) = traders priced reduced near-term physical disruption. **Assessment: POSITIONING UNWIND, NOT thesis-falsification.** Underlying disruption (EGA still offline, ALBA suspended, ~9% of global aluminum flow tied to Hormuz) is structurally intact. **If NYT preparation report operationalizes Monday, aluminum snaps back violently.** Crisis vs non-crisis split: ~80% crisis-trade-unwind / ~20% China-demand-skepticism. **Monday May 18 binary**: if NYT-priced gap-up in Brent on Monday open, aluminum re-prices Hormuz tail-risk premium back in. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor. **PRIOR (May 16):** LME ALUMINUM $3,791.50/mt -2.6% 1D / +3.9% 1M / +27.6% 3M — second-leg metals data**. Extending Thursday's -8.9% but at a more controlled pace; tariff overhang (50% Trump April clarification) + 4-yr-high long unwind continues; ~40% crisis-residual, ~60% non-crisis (tariff + China property + global industrial moderation). **The metals selloff Friday is now INFLATION-DRIVEN rather than geopolitically-driven** (markets dossier B3) — a different structural mechanism that points to a different recovery path. **The graph's 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift / below $4,400 sustained 5+ sessions = second-leg premium unwind' trigger added yesterday is tracking the bear-case at Day 1 of 5-session window** (gold $4,564, silver $77.16). **Silver -10.6% one-day cut Fortune intraday; -9.1% close — Goldman attribution: rising yields drove selloff + dual identity (monetary + industrial) made it hardest hit on rates shock + risk-off + dollar-bid combo; gold/silver ratio widened to 58.9.** Copper -4.8% (China stimulus skepticism + Trump-Xi tariff softening absence + risk-off cascade); Platinum -4.9% (precious-industrial complex hit on real-yield repricing + Trump-Xi disappointment); Palladium -2.3% (auto-demand weakness; industrial complex unwind). **EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor.** PRIOR (May 15): **C2 LME ALUMINUM -8.9% TO $3,545.75/mt — GEOPOLITICAL PREMIUM UNWIND, BIGGEST 1D DROP IN COMMODITY COMPLEX**. Primary chain (~95% crisis-linked): Aluminum had just hit four-year high $3,729.50 cash on May 13 on Hormuz/EGA supply premium (EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline, ~12-month restart); Trump-Xi joint statement May 14-15 'Strait of Hormuz must remain open' + China rare-earth export pause + tariff truce extension; market read this as the beginning of the war-discount unwind that Morgan Stanley had flagged; most-crowded long in base metals (cash +19.3% 3M, LME stocks at multi-year lows ~348-358kt); position liquidation violent off stretched long; SHFE roughly flat May 14 = confirms this is LME/Western unwind, not Shanghai-led. **Secondary contributor (~0.4%)**: No new supply news. No smelter restart, no Chinese export surge, no LME stockpile release. Physical supply is unchanged. **Assessment**: PURE REPRICING OF GEOPOLITICAL RISK PREMIUM following Trump-Xi Hormuz commitment. **Reversibility: HIGH for sentiment component; physical floor intact (EGA still offline)**. Any ceasefire violation, EGA restart slippage, or rare-earth/tariff backsliding re-inflates the premium quickly. Today's move is sentiment, not supply. **NEW TRIGGER PROPOSED**: aluminum sustains below $3,400 5+ sessions = second leg of unwind active. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor. **B/C3 LME ALUMINUM $3,624.50/mt (-5.6% 1D, +0.3% 1M, +22.0% 3M) — BIGGEST 1D DROP IN COMMODITY COMPLEX TODAY.** Drivers (~50% crisis, ~50% macro/demand): Position unwind + Discovery Alert '$3,559/t May 8 → $3,624 May 14' suggests cycle-low rebound got monetised; demand caution (Q2 industrial output softening) + USD bid. Gulf-supply premium UNWIND on Trump-Xi 'trade-not-war' opening + crisis-trade unwind across complex (Lockheed -15% MoM, CF -3.8%, Cheniere -2.0%, RBOB -4.5%, Frontline -3.5%). Per markets dossier B: 'positions long China-reflation (Cu +2.5%/Ag +2.6% prior session), short war-premium-supply (Al)' — today both retraced. **Copper $6.58/lb (-0.9% 1D, +8.3% 1M)** = trade-not-war narrative bid earlier retraced today; gold/silver/copper all mildly weaker = paper-physical bifurcation in metals complex reads as 'partial de-escalation pricing.' Gulf production damage unresolved (Borouge UAE, ALBA Bahrain, EGA Al Taweelah) but already in price. **Assessment: aluminum cleanest counter-signal to crisis premium re-rating — if war is truly tightening industrial-commodity supply broadly, aluminum should rally with copper. Instead BOTH retracing on Trump-Xi summit-pricing.** PRIOR (May 13): Aluminum had run to ~$3,600/mt 4-year high; current $3,510 = -2.5% from peak. **CANCELLED-WARRANT DECLINE -7% TO 26,500t SIGNALS REDUCED NEAR-TERM PHYSICAL DEMAND** — inventory queue shrinking, not deepening. Persian Gulf ~9% of global aluminum output — meaningful but not dominant; Gulf supply concerns ALREADY priced in +44% YoY level. **ASSESSMENT (markets dossier C8): Aluminum is cleanest counter-signal to fertilizer rally. If war were truly tightening industrial-commodity supply broadly, aluminum should rally with copper (+8.1% 1M, +11.9% 3M). Instead, aluminum showing DEMAND-SIDE SOFTNESS — likely China property + global industrial moderation.** **This is a signal that NOT ALL CRISIS-LINKED MOVES ARE CRISIS-DRIVEN; the global industrial cycle has its own tempo.** Watch for confirmation: if copper rolls over while gold stays muted, inflation/flow regime is shifting toward outright demand-softness regime — bad for cyclicals across the board. China May trade-data tape weakness (USD/CNY 6.79, Shanghai Composite -0.4%, property-sector weakness) contributes. Gulf production damage unresolved (Borouge UAE, ALBA Bahrain, EGA Al Taweelah) but already in price; ~10% crisis-residual, ~90% non-crisis demand-softness. Per markets.md D6: aluminum down -2.7% Apr 29 on profit-taking after April rally; Brent +6-8% to $118-120 should normally support Gulf-aluminum-supply-disruption thesis but profit-taking dominated. LME inventories contracted 0.58% to 381,050 tonnes — supply tight. Trade-policy normalization expectations. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since Apr 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk; physical-damage repair/restart 12-18 months structural. Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First reinforces structural thesis but day's tape was positioning-driven, not fundamentals. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): INFO-OPS-ADJACENT DIVERGENCE FLAGGED: Monday attribution to 'Iranian authorities stated that commercial vessels are now free to navigate the chokepoint following ongoing talks with the US' (Trading Economics/Mining.com) is OPPOSITE of the OPERATIONAL reality — 16 vessels Monday vs 138 baseline = 88% suppression; Sunday zero-transit. Someone trading aluminum read the soft Iran DIPLOMATIC quote and ignored AIS data. Also profit-taking after +11.5% 1M and $3,670 four-year high earlier in April + China export-increase expectations. Aluminum now CHEAP relative to operational reality if Wednesday ceasefire lapses — +$200-300/mt catch-down in 48-72hr. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since April 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

current.price: ~$3,480-3,600/mt -- 10 Jun LME 3-mo -2.9% to ~$3,480.5 (AlCircle); 11 Jun recovering toward ~$3,600 (TradingEconomics) on the Hormuz closure formalization. Contract/timestamp spread; direction = partial reversal of the -9.4% war-premium unwind.

current.driver: MOST REVERSIBLE MOVE ON THE BOARD, being tested exactly as flagged. The 10 Jun -2.9% was a demand-fear day inside a supply-constrained market (oil->inflation->policy->industrial-demand worry, ~60% crisis-derived macro). The 11 Jun recovery toward ~$3,600 on Iran's complete-closure declaration is the predicted mechanism: because the premium is REAL (genuine removed Gulf capacity -- EGA ~1-yr restart, ALBA partially suspended, WoodMac up to 3.5Mt 2026 loss), closure-formalization re-prices it UP where gold (no real premium) cannot bid. Copper resilient confirms premium dynamics, not demand collapse.

recent_signals.headline: **ALUMINUM AT A 4-YEAR HIGH ~$3,640-3,680 (+0.4% 1D, +2.6% MoM, +17.4% 3M) — DUAL DRIVER: GULF NAVAL BLOCKADE (~9% OF GLOBAL SUPPLY OFFLINE) + GUINEA BAUXITE EXPORT CONTROLS FROM JUNE. yfinance -13% 1D IS THE RECURRING LME-VINTAGE ARTIFACT — DISCARD.** (1) **Staleness correction**: the prior May 19 read (soft, falling toward $3,400 on China-demand skepticism) is OUT OF DATE — aluminum is at a 4-year high. (2) **The yfinance -13% 1D is a data artifact**: same LME-vintage / stale-comparator / continuous-contract-roll mechanism flagged May 19-20 (LME closed/stale comparator divides into a fresh print). The real 1D move is ~+0.4%. (3) **DUAL DRIVER (~50/50)**: (a) CRISIS — the Mideast naval blockade halts Gulf exports, taking ~9% of global aluminum supply offline (EGA Al Taweelah 1.6Mt, ALBA, Borouge unrepaired); (b) NON-CRISIS SUPPLY SHOCK — Guinea announced bauxite export controls starting June, hitting the upstream feedstock for alumina→aluminum. (4) **The key intelligence**: the Guinea overlay means part of the strength is structural and PERSISTS even on a Gulf/Hormuz deal — a deal removes the ~50% crisis premium but NOT the Guinea bauxite-control leg. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline (~12-month restart) remains the structural floor.
